original: Tibetan troops occupy Chang'an for 15 days, capital of the Chinese Tang Dynasty
Lotharius becomes King of France
Plymouth, England, becomes the first town incorporated by the English Parliament
The English Parliament re-establishes Catholicism
Castiliaans army occupies Zaragoza
Treaty of Xanten: Guliks-Kleefse War victory ends
Dutch troops under William of Orange occupy Bonn
Swedish king Charles XI establishes absolute monarchy
Ambrose Godfrey patents a gunpowder fueled fire extinguisher system in England
France & Bavaria renew secret treaty
General Washington forbids recruiting officers from enlisting black troops
The 1st Mayor of Paris and astronomer Jean Sylvain Bailly, is guillotined during the Reign of Terror
Andrew Ellicott makes the first known record of a meteor shower observation in the U.S, from a ship off the coast of Florida Keys
Allied troops occupy Zwolle, Netherlands
Great North Holland Canal (Amsterdam) opens
The great Leonid Meteor shower recorded
Sir James Young Simpson, a British physician, is the first to use chloroform as an anaesthetic
Jules Leotard performs 1st Flying Trapeze circus act (Paris) He also designed garment that bears his name
Bay District Race Track in San Francisco opens
Montreal & Britannia Football Clubs (QRFU) defeat Ontario Combined Team (ORFU) 3-0 in CRFU Championship game
Pudge Heffelfinger receives $500 to become first professional football player in the Allegheny Athletic Association's 4-0 win over the Pittsburgh Athletic Club
The treaty of the Durand Line is signed between present day Pakistan and Afghanistan - the Durand Line has gained international recognition as an international border between the two sister nations.
Lawrence Hargrave, Australian aeronautical pioneer and inventor of the box kite, linked four huge box kites together and flew - but remained attached to the ground by piano wire
British troops reach Durban, Natal
Exposition Universelle (World's Fair) in Paris closes after 50 million visitors - Art Nouveau style dominates
(November 12 & November 13) Norway holds a referendum in favor of monarchy over republic.
C W Gregory (NSW v Qld) starts day at 48*, is 366* at stumps
First possible movie stunt: A man jumps into the Hudson River from a burning balloon
Spanish Prime Minister José Canalejas y Méndez is assassinated by an anarchist book shopping in Madrid
Turks sultan Jamal Pasja declares a German holy war
Britain annexes Gilbert & Ellice archipelago in the Western Pacific
Theodore W. Richards is 1st American to win Nobel Prize in Chemistry
Emperor Karl of Austria-Hungary abdicates, Austria becomes a republic
Ross & Keith Smith start a 1 month flight from London to Australia
The Dalmatian coast between Italy and Yugoslavia is ceded to Yugoslavia
Washington Conference for Limitation of Armaments
The Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority is founded on the campus of Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana
Dr Hjalmar Schacht is appointed special commissioner to deal with the currency problem in Germany; by November 1924 the currency will have been restored
Jewish school Yeshivah Slobodka opens a branch in Hebron, Palestine
US & Italy sign peace accord about war debts
The first recorded aerial bombing on US soil took place in Williamson County, Illinois, during a feud between rival liquor gangs, the Sheltons and the Birgers
Notre Dame's Fighting Irish changes blue jerseys for green
British steamer "Vestris", sailing from NYC to Uruguay, capsizes and sinks off Hampton Roads, Virginia, kills 111, including many women and children
Maple Leaf Gardens opens in Toronto - Chic Blackhawks beat Leafs, 2-1
24 killed at Lancashire mine explosion
1st NFL football game on a Sunday at Philadelphia's Baker Bowl - sporting events on Sunday had been illegal on Sundays; Eagles tie Chicago Bears, 3-3
1st NFL football game on a Sunday at Pittsburgh's Forbes Field - sporting events on Sunday had been illegal on Sundays; Pirates lose to Brooklyn Dodgers 32-0
First known photo of so-called Loch Ness monster is taken by Hugh Gray
Nazis receive 92% of vote in German parliamentary elections
Egas Moniz performs the first modern brain surgery on the frontal lobes to treat mental disorders, at Santa Marta Hospital in Lisbon, Portugal
First-ever TV gardening show "In Your Garden" airs on the BBC
Oakland Bay Bridge opens
St Louis Browns sold to Donald L Barnes & William O DeWitt
Jews in Lodz, Poland, ordered to wear a yellow star of David
Blizzard strikes North American midwest, 154 die, including 69 on boat asail in the Great Lakes
Alma Heflin, 1st female test pilot for commercial aircraft - Piper Corporation, Lock Haven, Pennsylvania
Germany's drive to take Moscow halted
Radio stations WOV-AM & WNEW-AM in NYC swaps call letters
The Naval Battle of Guadalcanal begins between Allied and Japanese naval forces in the Solomon Islands; US force prevails after days of fighting
Landwacht (NSB-political party) forms in Netherlands
RAF sinks German battleship Tirpitz at Tromso Fjord, Norway
Nobel Peace Prize awarded to American politician Cordell Hull (for establishing the UN)
A branch of the Exchange National Bank in Chicago, Illinois opens the first ten drive-up teller windows
Walt Disney's "Song Of South" released
KPO-AM in San Francisco CA changes call letters to KNBC (now KNBR)
Schilderijenvervalser Han of Meegeren to 1 years jail sentenced
The first mobile betatron (particle accelerator) begins operation at the U.S. Naval Ordnance Laboratory, White Oak, Maryland
Gene Roberts sets NFL NY Giant rushing record (218 yds) vs Chic Cards
Philadelphia A's pitcher Bobby Shantz wins AL MVP
White Sox place Jim Rivera on 1 year probation after cleared of rape
US district Judge Grim, rules NFL can black out TV home games
Ellis Island, immigration station in NY Harbor is closed
1st West German officers sworn in

Doc and Marty McFly are sent "Back to the Future" when lightning strikes the Hill Valley clock tower, at exactly 10:04 p.m. generating the power to send the DeLorean and Marty back to 1985 [fictional]
Largest observed iceberg, 208 by 60 miles, 1st sighted
White Sox 2B Nellie Fox wins AL's MVP
Mercury-Redstone 1 test launch fails at 10 cm altitude
Jean becomes Grand Duke of Luxembourg
Paula Murphy sets female land speed record 226.37 MPH
General strike in Morocco against disappearance of Ben Barka
Mad Dog Vachon beats Crusher in Denver, to become NWA champ
Venera 2 launched by Soviet Union toward Venus
18-year-old US High schooler Robert Smith kills 5 people at Arizona's Rose-Mar Beauty College for fame
Dick The Bruiser beats Mad Dog Vachon in Omaha, to become NWA champ
Dodgers complete an 18-game tour of Japan with a 9-8-1 record
Packers' Travis Williams returns 2 kickoffs for TDs against Browns, setting largest margin of Browns defeat (48), winning 55-7
KSEL (now KAMC) TV channel 28 in Lubbock, TX (ABC) begins broadcasting
US Supreme Court: Epperson v. Arkansas declares unconstitutional the Arkansas law banning the teaching of evolution in public schools
US army announces investigating William Calley for alleged massacre of civilians at Vietnamese village of My Lai in March, 19
WJJY (now WJPT) TV channel 14 in Jacksonville, IL (ABC) 1st broadcast
Cleveland Cavaliers notch 1st NBA victory, in their 16th game, beating the Trail Blazers 105-103, in Portland, Oregon
Cyclone Bhola makes landfall in East Pakistan (now Bangladesh), killing up to 500,000 people, making it the deadliest tropical cyclone ever recorded
Scientists perform 1st artificial synthesis of a live cell
The Northern Ireland Housing Executive (NIHE) is formed; the NIHE gradually took over control of the building and allocation of public sector housing in Northern Ireland

Charity concert for United Crusade (now United Way) in San Diego, California attracts nearly 52,000; performers include: J. Geils Band, Jesse Colin Young, Quicksilver Messenger Service, Foghat, and Dr. Hook and the Medicine Show
British sitcom series "Last of the Summer Wine" debuts on BBC; it goes on to become the longest running sitcom ever, airing for 31 seasons
A salmon is discovered in the River Thames, England, for the first time since 1833
South Africa suspended from UN General Assembly over racial policies
American Supreme Court Justice William O Douglas (77) retires after 36 years
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
New Orleans elects 1st black mayor, Ernest "Dutch" Morial
Tony Franklin of Philadelphia Eagles kicks 59-yard field goal
US President Carter announces a halts to Iranian oil imports & freezes Iranian assets
Baltimore's Steve Stone wins AL Cy Young Award
US space probe Voyager I approaches 77,000-mi (124,000 km) of Saturn
1st balloon crossing of Pacific is completed (Double Eagle V)
2nd shuttle mission-1st time spacecraft launched twice (Columbia 2)
Bill C Davis' "Mass Appeal" premieres in NYC
Great Britain performs nuclear test
Aaron Pryor scores a 14th round KO of Alexis Argüello in Miami to retain his WBA super lightweight boxing title in the first of 2 meetings between the Hall of Famers; The Ring’s 'Fight of the Decade'
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
4 die in a train crash in Marshall Texas
NJ Devils 1st overtime game, lose to Calgary Flames 4-3
NBC premiere of "Victims for Victims: The Theresa Saldana Story", fact based telepic of actress Saldana's near fatal attack and its aftermath
Space shuttle astronauts snared a satellite 1st space salvage
Secretary in Ann Arbor Mich wounded by package bomb
STS 61-B vehicle moves to launch pad
France performs nuclear test
Heavy snow closes schools from DC to Maine
First commercial bungy jumping company begins operating near Queenstown, New Zealand, run by A. J. Hackett
Japan beats MLB All-Star team 5-4 in Tokyo (Game 6 of 7)
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Brazil holds its 1st free presidential election in 29 years
George Forrest's musical "Grand Hotel" opens at the Martin Beck Theatre in NYC, runs for 1,018 performances, and wins five Tony Awards

"The Body Bag Game" - after Eagles coachBuddy Ryanthreatens beating Redskins so badly "they'll have to be carted off in body bags" the Eagles defense score 3 touchdowns winning 28–14, knocking 8 Redskins out of the game
Dili Massacre, Indonesian forces open fire on a crowd of protesters in Dili, East Timor, killing at least 250
British TV comedy "Absolutely Fabulous" written by Jennifer Saunders, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley first appears on the BBC
NY Yankee pitcher Steve Howe is reinstated for 8th time
Marino breaks Tarkenton's NFL all-time passing yardage mark of 47,003
NY MTA raises subway & bus fares from $1.25 to $1.50
STS 74 (Atlantis 15), launches into orbit
Saudi Arabian Flight 763 and Kazakhstan Airlines Flight 1907 collide in midair over Charkhi Dadri in India, killing 349 people, the third-deadliest aviation disaster in history
Toronto's Pat Hentgen wins AL Cy Young Award
Dick Vitale signs with ESPN through year 2004
NY Islanders tie Detroit Red Wings 1-1, to end 10 game losing streak
Düzce earthquake strikes Turkey with a magnitude of 7.2, killing 845 people and causing widespread destruction

American Airlines Flight 587, an Airbus A300 on its way to the Dominican Republic, crashes minutes after takeoff from John F. Kennedy International Airport in NYC, killing all 260 on board and five on the ground
Taliban forces abandon Afghanistan's capital Kabul, ahead of advancing Afghan Northern Alliance troops
In Nasiriya, Iraq, at least 23 people, including the first Italian casualties of the 2003 Iraq War, are killed in a suicide bomb attack on an Italian police base
Shanghai Transrapid sets a new world record for commercial railway systems with a speed of 501 km/h (311 mph)
Former Soviet republic of South Ossetia holds a second referendum on independence from Georgia
Opera "Silent Night" by Kevin Puts opens at the Ordway Theater, St. Paul sung in English, German, French, Italian and Latin (winner 2012 Pulitzer Prize for Music)
Into the Silence by Wade Davis wins the 2012 Samuel Johnson Prize
European Space Agency's Rosetta lands the Philae probe on the comet 67P/Churyumov-Gerasimenko
Suicide bombings in Lebanon kill 43, Isis claim responsibility
7.3 earthquake hits near Halabjal, Iraq, on border with Iran, killing over 500 people, with 7,000 injured
Israeli secret operation in Gaza kills eight including one Israeli and an Hamas Commander, igniting tensions with retaliatory rocket attack from Gaza
Disney launches its film and television streaming service Disney+

Former Houston Astros MLB pitcher Mike Fiers reveals team secretly “stole signs” via camera from visiting teams 2015-17; comms between opposing pitchers & catchers intercepted and relayed to Astros batters
Israeli forces kill senior commander of Palestinian Islamic Jihad militant group in the Gaza Strip with Palestinian militants setting off retaliatory rockets at Israel
At least 68 inmates killed in new fighting at Ecuadorean prison, the Litoral Penitentiary, Guayaquil, following earlier violence in September
New Zealand's Black Ferns win Women's World Rugby Championship defeating England 34-31 at Eden Park in Auckland in front of a capacity crowd
Two vintage aircraft crash in midair at the Wings Over Dallas WWII Airshow, killing all six people
US Mid-term elections: Democrats retain control of the Senate with Catherine Cortez Masto's victory in Nevada 50-49, with run-off in Georgia still to be decided
Unprecedented heatwave in Brazil affects more than 3,000 towns with Rio de Janeiro reaching 42.5C - very high humidity makes it feel like 58.5C
WHO calls for attacks on and around hospitals in Gaza to stop, with newborn babies reportedly dying as a result of power, water and oxygen cuts
"Orbital" by British writer Samantha Harvey, set on the International Space Station, wins the 2024 Booker Prize for fiction
Former Massachusetts Air National Guard Jack Teixeira is sentenced to 15 years in prison for stealing classified information from the Pentagon and sharing it online
The Archbishop of Canterbury, Justin Welby, resigns in the wake of a report into a church sex abuser that said he should have notified him to police
US State Department confirms over 10,000 North Korea troops have joined Russian forces in the Kursk region to fight Ukraine in a major escalation of the war
Duke of BurgundyCharles the Boldoccupies and plunders Liège
Robert Hookeappointed Curator of Experiments to the Royal Society, England
Meeting of the Royal Society appointsJames Cookas head of an expedition to the Pacific Ocean to observe the transit of Venus on Tahiti in 1769 (will be Cook's 1st voyage aboard HMS Endeavour)
Mabel Loomis Todd publishes the first edition of "Poems byEmily Dickinson"
Andrew Fisherassumes the position of prime minister for what turns out to be a short-lived second Labour government, Australia
Politician William LyonMackenzie Kingtakes his seat in the House of Commons as Canda's first Minister of Labour
British explorerRobert Falcon Scott's diary and body are found in Antarctica
JudgeKenesaw Mountain Landiselected first baseball commissioner by team owners
In Germany,Adolf Hitleris arrested for attempt to seize power during "Beer Hall Putsch" coup

Jean Sibeliusand Ashton's ballet "Lady of Shalott" premieres in London
Nobel Prize for literature awarded to American playwrightEugene O'Neill
Hermann Goeringannounces he wants Madagascar as a Jewish homeland
The SS Robert E. Peary, a Liberty ship built in 4 days and 15.5 hours atHenry J. Kaiser's Richmond Shipyard #2, is launched, setting a new record
Japanese Prime MinisterHideki Tojosentenced to death by war crimes tribunal
David Ben-Gurion, resigns as Prime Minister of Israel
Lee Kuan Yewand others form the People's Action Party (PAP) in Singapore to work towards self-rule in Singapore

Military coup against Republic of Vietnamese PresidentNgô Đình Diệmfails
Ferdinand Marcoselected 10th President of the Philippines
Al Pacinostars in "Deadly Circle of Violence", an episode of the ABC television series NYPD
AuthorAleksandr Solzhenitsynexpelled from the Soviet Writers Union

Paul McCartneyreleases single "We All Stand Together"
Roger Clemenswins AL Cy Young Award unanimously
Crown PrinceAkihitois formally installed as Emperor Akihito of Japan, becoming the 125th Japanese monarch.
The World Wide Web is first proposed by CERN computer scientistsTim Berners-Leeand Robert Cailliau
Atlanta BraveTom Glavinewins NL Cy Young Award
US TV series "Full House" 100th episode, the twins are born
German Benetton driverMichael Schumachercrashes in the season-ending Australian Grand Prix in Adelaide but claims second straight F1 World Drivers Championship by 33 points from Damon Hill
Vice President of the United States,Al Gore, symbolically signs the Kyoto Protocol, but the US never ratifies it
Belgian tennis star Justine Hénin-Hardenne wins WTA Tour Championship title decider in Madrid, Spain; beats defending championAmélie Mauresmo6-4, 6-3

Joe Sakic, Mats Sundin,Pavel Bureand Adam Oates are inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame
Novak Đokovićof Serbia wins his second season-ending ATP World Tour Finals tennis title beating 6-time championRoger Federer7-6, 7-5 in the final in London. England
Out Magazine namesBarack Obama'Ally of the Year',Obamabecomes first sitting US President to pose for cover of a gay magazine
Jackie Chanawarded honorary Oscar for his film achievements at 8th Annual Governors Awards ceremony
For the first timePrince Charleslays the wreath to Great Britain's war dead, replacing Queen Elizabeth

550th anniversary of the birth ofGuru Nanak- the founder of Sikhism celebrated in India and Pakistan
Former Bolivian PresidentEvo Moralestakes asylum in Mexico
Space western TV series set in the Star Wars franchise "TheMandalorian" byJon Favreauis released on Disney+
US President-electJoe Bidenannounces Ron Klain will be his White House Chief of Staff
"Voyage",Abba's first album of new material in 40 years, tops the UK album chart
LA judge rules to endBritney Spears' conservatorship, which had controlled almost all aspects of her life for 14 years
Taylor Swiftreleases her directorial debut the short film "All Too Well" alongside her re-recorded album "Red (Taylor's Version)"
Amazon founderJeff Bezosannounces his 3rd 'Courage and Civility Award' of $100M to country singer-songwriterDolly Partonto distribute to charities of her choice
Actor and directorJohn Krasinskiis named People Magazine's "Sexiest Man Alive" for 2024
President-electDonald Trumpselects entrepreneurElon Muskand former presidential candidate Vivek Ramaswamy, to head a newly-created Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)
